description:
Goal: canonical activation keys should read stable definition facts instead of peeking through lowered bodies on every activation. Signal: Recursive(fn) correctly detects direct, mutual, and lambda-mediated recursion, DispatchMask(fn) is derived from the entry-dispatch plan once, canonical keying no longer rescans bodies per call, and the ignored quicksort JSONL dump harness remains available for deep inspection. Strategy: add facts/jobs for Recursive(fn) and DispatchMask(fn), compute recursion as self-reachability over the lowered static call graph with lambda-instantiation edges, make canonical_activation_key consume those facts after LoweredBody/EntryDispatch are available, and keep the trace dump harness disabled by default.

Change:
- Add Recursive(function) and DispatchMask(function) fact families.
- Derive DispatchMask from PatternDispatchPlan input usage.
- Derive Recursive from the lowered static call graph, including Lambda owner-to-generated-function edges and generated-function outgoing calls.
- Treat abstract protocol callbacks as terminal static edges; semantic protocol resolution owns the selected implementation path.
- Replace function_is_recursive and per-activate dispatch-mask recomputation with fact reads.
- Keep an ignored quicksort JSONL telemetry dump test for occasional full-trace inspection.

Why:
- The current detector only sees direct self-calls and if branches, so closure-mediated recursion can miss the convergence gate.
- Recursion and dispatch masks are stable properties of frozen lowered/dispatch facts; computing them on every activation is the wrong ownership boundary.

Checks:
- A closure-recursion repro gates recursive non-dispatch inputs correctly.
- Quicksort and Enum.reduce semantic closures remain unchanged.
- canonical_activation_key does not walk lowered bodies or dispatch plans.
- The ignored telemetry dump test can be run explicitly without entering the default suite.
title: Compiler2 semantics: derive recursion and dispatch-key facts
